articles.title: Target’s Mid-Market Position Leaves Margins Vulnerable Amid Mix and Price Pressure
articles.summary: Target's business strategy focuses on leveraging its store base for shopping and fulfillment, supported by a mix of discretionary and staple categories. However, the company's reliance on operational execution rather than structural advantage leaves its margins vulnerable to competition and price pressures. Over the next five years, Target plans to enhance supply chain efficiency, digital capabilities, and private-label development to defend its market share.
